purpose: Best-effort foreground window acquisition: combines WinForms Activate/BringToFront, a TopMost toggle trick, and AttachThreadInput+SetForegroundWindow for maximum reliability across Windows policies.
calls_out_to: user32!AttachThreadInput — temporarily attaches UI thread to foreground window's thread
invariants: BestEffortBringToFront restores TopMost to desiredTopMost after the brief bump
All Win32 calls are individually try/catched — never throws
gotchas: AttachThreadInput is a known Windows hack; attach+detach must be paired — the finally block ensures cleanup even if SetForegroundWindow fails
Windows may still refuse focus stealing if the user is actively typing in another process — this is OS-by-design, not a bug
